title: GSR 278 (E) The Railways (Punitive charges for overloading of wagon) Amendment Rules, 2018
description: GSR 278 (E) The Railways (Punitive charges for overloading of wagon) Amendment Rules, 2018

ARS AGHA Fels AAA A Ha ca F alah Ae Slat H ATA F Aq Sale AAA SF ale Woy ah ATO AK H fers SeTHH TAL Teer PAT STAT.” [war. a. chatsre/1394/201 1/04] Use ATA, HAH aH, AAA ATMS (FX) feof: aa faa ararf.570(@) fears 17 werd, 2012 art aa * WTA Aaa, aM Il Gs 3, Sy-@e (i) H ware feu we ft aur afeaaat dear arar.f4.898(s), feate 17 fearax, 2012 wa ar.anr.fa. 550(S), feat 10 ters, 2015 ater aartfera feu ae MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS (Railway Board) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 26th March, 2018 G.S.R. 278(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (1) read with clause (d) of sub-section (2) of Section 87 of the Railways Act, 1989 (No. 24 of 1989), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Railways (Punitive charges for overloading of wagon) Rules, 2012, namely:- 1. (1) These rules may be called the Railways (Punitive charges for overloading of wagon) Amendment Rules, 2018. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Inthe Railways (Punitive charges for overloading of wagon) Rules, 2012, in the SCHEDULE,- (a) under the heading Situation-A, in Part-II, for item (ii), the following item shall be substituted, namely:- “Gi) For BCCW wagons Extent of overloading *Punitive charges leviable on the entire weight of the commodity loaded in excess of permissible carrying capacity and loading tolerance of half tonne If the weight of the commodity exceeds the permissible carrying capacity of wagon: (a) up to half tonne Nil. (b) by more than half tonne but not more than three and | Two times of the freight rate applicable to that commodity. half tonnes -- 2 of 3 -- [AM 1—-arS 3(i)] UR Hl UIT : AMAR 3 Extent of overloading *Punitive charges leviable on the entire weight of the commodity loaded in excess of permissible carrying capacity and loading tolerance of half tonne (c) by more than three and half tonnes Three times of the freight rate applicable to the highest class. *Explanation.- It is hereby clarified that on the weight exceeding the permissible carrying capacity upto half tonne, the normal freight at the rate applicable to the commodity shall be recoverable and punitive charges shall be recovered for the entire weight of the commodity in excess of permissible carrying capacity and loading tolerance of half tonne.” (b) under the heading Situation-B, in Part-II, for item(ii), the following item shall be substituted, namely:- “Gi) For BCCW wagons Extent of overloading *Punitive charges leviable on the entire weight of the commodity loaded in excess of permissible carrying capacity If the weight of the commodity exceeds the permissible carrying capacity of wagon: (a) up to half tonne Nil. (b) by more than half tonne but not more than three and half tonnes Three times of the freight rate applicable to the highest class. (c) by more than three and half tonnes Five times of the freight rate applicable to the highest class. *Explanation.- It is hereby clarified that on the weight exceeding the permissible carrying capacity upto half tonne, the normal freight at the rate applicable to the commodity shall be recoverable. However, in case of weight exceeds the permissible carrying capacity by more than half tonne, punitive charges shall be recovered for the entire weight of the commodity in excess of permissible carrying capacity.” [F.N. TCR/1394/201 1/04] RAHUL AGARWAL, Executive Director, Traffic Commercial (Rates) Note: The principal rules were published in the Gazette of India Extraordinary, Part If Section 3, Sub-section (i) vide number G.S.R. 570(E), dated the 17° July, 2012 and amended vide notification numbers G.S.R. 898(E), dated the 17 December, 2012 and G.S.R. 550(B), dated the 10" July, 2015.
